Strip_Summary: Dark alley POV. Body camera red light switching off. Seventeen seconds begin.
Cop stickman standing over civilian under single buzzing streetlight in dark alley.
Bold text: 'Not the one on the street. The one you create.'
POV hands tapping bag through civilian pocket. Casual gesture. Done a hundred times.
POV hand reaching in pulling out small bag. Holding up to dash camera lens.
Bold text: 'You don't find evidence. You place it.'
Academy graduation ceremony. Fresh-uniformed stickman receiving badge. Mother with camera nearby.
Stickman pinning badge on uniform. Badge has weight. Like every arrest will matter.
Patrol car lot. Protect and serve lettering on car doors. Stickman believes system works.
Stickman running thumb across badge one last time before getting in patrol car.
Courtroom. Stickman watching guilty man walk out through courthouse doors.
Prosecutor at table not making eye contact. Judge on bench saying something inaudible.
Man pushing door open with one hand. Cop stickman standing in gallery seats.
Stickman with hands in pockets. Courthouse hallway. Floor wax smell. Sandwich on bench.
Stickman walking past people. Nobody looking up. Security guard by elevator not noticing.
Patrol car interior at night. Partner stickman (faceless) eyes forward. Both hands on wheel.
Bold text: 'This isn't how it works.'
Patrol car interior. Radio crackling. Streetlights sliding past window one by one.
Stickman looking out car window. Something about the way partner said it. Waiting to.
Dark alley. Partner stickman (faceless) reaching into civilian pocket. Pulling out something new.
Civilian face hitting car hood. Metal on bone. Sound before understanding.
Cop stickman standing three feet behind partner. Close enough to see bag in hand.
Stickman saying nothing. Partner not looking back. Doesn't need to.
Bold text: 'Your silence is the first yes.'
Night street. First time. Suspect stickman (faceless hoodie) guilty. Everyone knows.
Evidence not there. Not enough for court. Cop stickman reaching toward pocket.
Small bag placed in right pocket with one hand. Other hand holds shoulder steady.
Cop stickman making evidence match truth. Noticing hands are steady. Not shaking.
Close-up of stickman hands. Completely steady. Not even a little.
Precinct desk. Nothing happens. No questions asked. No report flagged.
Filing paperwork at desk. Same form. Precinct smelling like old coffee and printer ink.
Fluorescent light flickering overhead. Nobody reading over shoulder. Nobody ever does.
Stickman at desk realizing it works. No one checking. No one ever checking.
Stickman alone at precinct desk. System not catching because not wanting to.
Sergeant office. Door open. Sergeant stickman (faceless wider) shaking cop hand.
Commendation letter warm from printer. Being folded and placed in locker.
Closing locker door. Blurry reflection in metal surface. Standing there a second.
Bold text: 'The system doesn't just stay silent. It rewards you.'
Montage progression. Second plant easier. Third without thinking.
Body camera check. Already off. Don't remember turning it off. But it's off.
Precinct desk close-up. Pen writing report. What happened except it didn't. Report says it did.
Bold text: 'Ink makes it real.'
Written means happened. Nobody made the rule. Everyone follows. Cop at desk with files.
Patrol car. Camera off reflex like blinking. Not hearing the click anymore.
Seventeen seconds enough. Sometimes twelve. Not rushing. Ritual precision.
Saying stop resisting before movement. Audio recording. Evidence is voice on recording.
Left: courtroom testimony professional. Right: precinct quota board with name circled near top.
Name on board circled. Sergeant leaning on doorframe asking about numbers dipping.
Stickman turning away from sergeant. Not answering. Just going back out.
Stickman in patrol car alone. Thinking about saying something. Not saying it.
Stop noticing the difference. The loop begins. Routine settling.
Four-panel split: choose target - prepare story - plant evidence - swear oath.
Same four steps every night. Same gloves. Eating granola bar between steps in car.
Wrapper under seat with others. That should bother you. It doesn't.
Stickman staring forward in patrol car. Don't remember when it stopped being a decision.
Bold text: 'You don't pick criminals. You pick people no one will defend.'
No phone. No lawyer. No one waiting at home. Target selection criteria.
Scanning block from patrol car. Windows down. Picking the one standing alone in hoodie.
Complaint goes to own precinct. Your precinct. Target not seeing you coming.
Patrol car interior. Partner saw everything. Talking about the game. About lunch.
Silence between conversations holds truth. Public defender not reading the file.
Defender carrying stack of folders. Half are yours. Judge nodding because uniform.
Three words close every question. Courtroom cold. Air conditioning humming.
Nobody objecting. Nobody asking follow-up. System too tired to check.
Walking out of courthouse into parking lot. Warm air hitting. Standing holding keys.
Stickman standing still in parking lot. Realizing that is the point. Always was.
Precinct. Promotion board. Name on list. Coffee in hand. Every arrest manufactured.
Not one real case. Pen mark next to name. Unknown who put it there.
Teaching rookie the method. Same patrol car. Same route. Same posture from three years ago.
Stickman in patrol car saying it same way partner once said it to him.
Bold text: 'This is how it works.'
Rookie stickman (faceless shorter) nodding. Not asking. You had it too.
Stop checking first. Used to matter guilty or not. Tonight is different.
Person against wall. Innocent. Hands up palms open shaking. Close-up of vulnerable pose.
Grocery bag on ground. Milk and something in a box. They were walking home.
Thumb moving to vest pocket. Fingers hovering half second. Then closing. Doing it anyway.
Same motion. Same pocket. Same seventeen seconds. Ritual completion.
Bold text: 'You don't find evidence. You place it.'
Bold text: 'Same words. Different weight.'
Internal Affairs calling Thursday. Partner calling first. Forty minutes before IA.
Told what to say. Writing on napkin in break room. Throwing napkin away.
IA interview room. Twenty-two minutes counted. Institutional fluorescent lighting.
Same questions three ways. Same answers three ways. Woman across table writing without showing.
Routine. Standard. Nothing to see. It works every time.
Night drive through neighborhood. Arrested person standing on corner. Same spot. Same clothes.
They see the patrol car. They don't run. Just standing there.
They just looking. Keep driving. Foot not changing on gas.
Rearview mirror. Their face getting smaller and smaller.
Not caught. Not punished. Investigation closed. Two-page file. Stamp at bottom.
System protects itself because you are the system. Stickman standing alone.
Training officer now. Same desk partner sat at. Cracked armrest on left side.
Coffee stain on desk corner. Or a different one. Can't tell which.
Rookies learning method. Same hesitation. First time watching.
Same silence after. Same look in car. One mouth open about to ask.
He doesn't ask. Don't want him to. Three months later doing it alone.
Filing paperwork after. Same form. Same pen. Same desk.
Bold text: 'The method is the department. The department is the method.'
Stickman alone at desk late. Everyone gone home. Arrest records spread out by date.
Trying to remember one real arrest. Evidence already there before getting to it.
Flipping through manila folders. Blue ink. Black ink. Real and fake read the same.
Same handwriting. Same forms. Same box checked on line fourteen.
Pages blurring. Dates running into names running into case numbers. Then just files again.
Bold text: 'You can't.'
Stickman closing folder on desk. Walking out. Hallway light buzzing behind.
Mirror of HOOK. Camera turning off. Seventeen seconds. All it ever took.
Bold text: 'You don't find evidence. You place it.'
Same alley. Same click. Same hands. But older now. Grey temples. Lines around eyes.
Bold text: 'They mean what you are. They always did.'
Bold text: 'You can't tell which ones were real. You're not sure there were any.'
